name: Adyghe
notes: Adyghe (адыгабзэ, West Circassian) — Northwest Caucasian language of the Republic of Adygea and the Circassian diaspora. Written in a Cyrillic-based alphabet (an Arabic-script orthography is used by some diaspora communities). GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED: the Adyghe Cyrillic alphabet encodes a very large consonant inventory (ejectives, labialised and pharyngealised series) through multi-character digraphs and trigraphs (е.g. ку, къу, гъу, лӏ, шӏу), which requires dedicated research to map reliably. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in the standard Adyghe Cyrillic orthography.
